children.heading: Inclusion of Tarpon Basin property
children.label: a Inclusion of Tarpon Basin property

children.heading: Hurricane Hole
children.label: A Hurricane Hole
children.text: The term “Hurricane Hole” means the natural salt-water body of water within the Duesenbury Tracts of the eastern parcel of the Tarpon Basin boundary adjustment and accessed by Duesenbury Creek.

children.heading: Map
children.label: B Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Proposed Tarpon Basin Boundary Revision”, numbered 160/80,012, and dated May 2008.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: C Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: i
children.text: is comprised of approximately 600 acres of land and water surrounding Hurricane Hole, as generally depicted on the map; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is located in South Key Largo.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The boundary of the Everglades National Park is adjusted to include the Tarpon Basin property.

children.heading: Acquisition authority
children.label: B Acquisition authority
children.text: The Secretary may acquire from willing sellers by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange, land, water, or interests in land and water, within the area depicted on the map, to be added to Everglades National Park.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: C Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: D Administration
children.text: Land added to Everglades National Park by this section shall be administered as part of Everglades National Park in accordance with applicable laws (including regulations).

children.heading: Hurricane Hole
children.label: 3 Hurricane Hole
children.text: The Secretary may allow use of Hurricane Hole by sailing vessels during emergencies, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Company
children.label: A Company
children.text: The term “Company” means Florida Power & Light Company.

children.label: i
children.text: owned by the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: administered by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: located within the National Park; and

children.label: I
children.text: Tract A, which is adjacent to the Tamiami Trail, U.S. Rt. 41; and

children.label: II
children.text: Tract B, which is located on the eastern boundary of the National Park.

children.heading: Map
children.label: C Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map prepared by the National Park Service, entitled “Proposed Land Exchanges, Everglades National Park”, numbered 160/60411A, and dated September 2008.

children.heading: National Park
children.label: D National Park
children.text: The term “National Park” means the Everglades National Park located in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: is owned by the State, the specific area and location of which shall be determined by the State; or

children.label: I
children.text: is owned by the Company;

children.label: II
children.text: comprises approximately 320 acres; and

children.label: III
children.text: is located within the East Everglades Acquisition Area, as generally depicted on the map as “Tract D”.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: F Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: G State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Florida and political subdivisions of the State, including the South Florida Water Management District.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the provisions of this paragraph, if the State offers to convey to the Secretary all right, title, and interest of the State in and to specific parcels of non-Federal land, and the offer is acceptable to the Secretary, the Secretary may, subject to valid existing rights, accept the offer and convey to the State all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the Federal land generally depicted on the map as “Tract A”.

children.heading: Conditions
children.label: B Conditions
children.text: The land exchange under subparagraph (A) shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The values of the land involved in the land exchange under subparagraph (A) shall be equal.

children.heading: Equalization
children.label: ii Equalization
children.text: If the values of the land are not equal, the values may be equalized by donation, payment using donated or appropriated funds, or the conveyance of additional parcels of land.

children.heading: Appraisals
children.label: D Appraisals
children.text: Before the exchange of land under subparagraph (A), appraisals for the Federal and non-Federal land shall be conducted in accordance with the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions and the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice.

children.heading: Technical corrections
children.label: E Technical corrections
children.text: Subject to the agreement of the State, the Secretary may make minor corrections to correct technical and clerical errors in the legal descriptions of the Federal and non-Federal land and minor adjustments to the boundaries of the Federal and non-Federal land.

children.heading: Administration of land acquired by Secretary
children.label: F Administration of land acquired by Secretary
i
children.text: become part of the National Park; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be administered in accordance with the laws applicable to the National Park System.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the provisions of this paragraph, if the Company offers to convey to the Secretary all right, title, and interest of the Company in and to the non-Federal land generally depicted on the map as “Tract D”, and the offer is acceptable to the Secretary, the Secretary may, subject to valid existing rights, accept the offer and convey to the Company all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the Federal land generally depicted on the map as “Tract B”, along with a perpetual easement on a corridor of land contiguous to Tract B for the purpose of vegetation management.

children.heading: Conditions
children.label: B Conditions
children.text: The land exchange under subparagraph (A) shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The values of the land involved in the land exchange under subparagraph (A) shall be equal unless the non-Federal land is of higher value than the Federal land.

children.heading: Equalization
children.label: ii Equalization
children.text: If the values of the land are not equal, the values may be equalized by donation, payment using donated or appropriated funds, or the conveyance of additional parcels of land.

children.heading: Appraisal
children.label: D Appraisal
children.text: Before the exchange of land under subparagraph (A), appraisals for the Federal and non-Federal land shall be conducted in accordance with the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions and the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice.

children.heading: Technical corrections
children.label: E Technical corrections
children.text: Subject to the agreement of the Company, the Secretary may make minor corrections to correct technical and clerical errors in the legal descriptions of the Federal and non-Federal land and minor adjustments to the boundaries of the Federal and non-Federal land.

children.heading: Administration of land acquired by Secretary
children.label: F Administration of land acquired by Secretary
i
children.text: become part of the National Park; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be administered in accordance with the laws applicable to the National Park System.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 4 Map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Boundary revision
children.label: 5 Boundary revision
children.text: On completion of the land exchanges authorized by this subsection, the Secretary shall adjust the boundary of the National Park accordingly, including removing the land conveyed out of Federal ownership.
